hook: District 14 condos are printing 5%+ yields while D10 landlords flex $62k rents

slides.title: Where Rental ROI Wins
slides.subtitle: Q1 2026 condo rental data: which districts beat 5% net and which ones just look premium

slides.headline: Nassim Road detached: Singapore's rental ceiling
slides.footnote: Single detached house lease, Nassim Road, District 10, Jan 2026. URA rental data 26Q1.

slides.title: Avg Condo Rent vs Yield Tier

items.label: D14 · Sims Drive

slides.footnote: URA rental caveats 26Q1 (Jan–Mar 2026). Yield estimates use indicative market capital values.

slides.headline: Sims Urban Oasis 2BR: rent-to-price ratio leads the pack
slides.footnote: Median 2BR rent across 20+ Sims Urban Oasis leases, Q1 2026. URA data 26Q1.

slides.title: 3 Rental Truths From Q1 2026
items: District 14 (Sims Drive) logged 20+ leases in Q1 — high transaction velocity signals strong tenant demand and low void risk for landlords.
District 2 (Wallich Residence) 1BR rents hit $7,500/mo in Mar vs $7,000 in Jan — ultra-luxury downtown units are still pushing upward.
District 15 (Tanjong Rhu) 2BR rents range $3,800–$5,000: wide spread means unit condition and floor level materially move your yield.
District 25 (Woodlands) 3BR at $4,600/mo on lower capital values — North region remains the quiet yield play most investors overlook.
Nassim Road detached at $62,000/mo confirms landed GCB-adjacent stock operates in a category entirely separate from condo yield math.

slides.tag: Wallich vs Sims · D02 vs D14
slides.title: Absolute Rent vs Yield: The Trade-off

items.label: Wallich 4BR · D02
items.sub: Highest non-landed lease in dataset; capital value ~$6M+ means gross yield ~3.6%

items.label: Wallich 1BR · D02
items.sub: Mar 2026 lease; smallest unit, strongest $/sqft rental rate in D02

items.label: Sims 3BR · D14
items.sub: 800–900 sqft; multiple leases at this level — repeatable, not a one-off

items.label: Sims 1BR · D14
items.sub: 400–500 sqft; entry-level unit on sub-$700k purchase = yield story strongest here

slides.footnote: URA rental caveats 26Q1. Capital value estimates based on recent URA resale transaction benchmarks.

slides.title: Chase Yield? Look Past The Postcode
slides.body: D10 landlords chase prestige but D14 and D25 landlords are quietly banking 5%+ gross on condos bought under $1.2M. The Wallich and Shangri-La numbers make headlines — Sims Drive makes money. In this rate environment, net yield over 4.5% beats capital-gains speculation every time.
slides.quote: The best rental investment isn't the one everyone talks about — it's the one that pays you every month.

caption:
📈 D14 condos are quietly delivering 5%+ yields while D10 grabs the headlines.

• Sims Urban Oasis 2BR median rent: $3,900/mo across 20+ Q1 2026 leases
• Wallich Residence 4BR hits $18,000/mo — but capital values crush net yield below 4%
• District 25 (Woodlands) 3BR at $4,600/mo on low entry price = the overlooked ROI play

If your investment goal is cash flow, stop chasing postcodes and start reading the yield math.
